programme.desc: Randy and Cody are wrapping things up on the Raven before bow season arrives in Missouri. Planting I-70 the plot where Randy killed one of his biggest Missouri bucks ever last year. Then Randy and Cody attempt to setup a new farm they are calling “The River Farm” they attempt to plant a food plot, bush hog, spray the plot, setup a new Titan blind, and setup a Hooyman dual perimeter fence all in one day before the sun goes down.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Randy is getting the last food plot of the season in down at the Dirty Bird in Oklahoma. Then opening day of the 2025 bow season starts off in an unusual way for the crew. Randy smokes a Hog and Nate Has a really close call with one of the target bucks.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Trenton makes a trip to South Carolina to fish Lake Murray. He meets up with the Rather Outdoors crew to spend time on the water and at the Rather HQ checking out all the gear from Lews and Strike King.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: The Crew is chasing Tarpon in Tampa Florida with Ryan Harrington. After a few close calls and grinding out the long days, they finally catch up to the Silver King.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: The Southards continue their family tradition on Table Rock Lake. While they’re there, Trenton spends a day with “Mr. Worldwide” James Watson and learns some things from one of the best anglers on the lake.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: With a free day before Icast. Trenton and Ryan go offshore to try and fill the cooler. Catching a variety of species and hoping to get their hands on a hogfish.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark Peterson sets his sights on a Free-Range New Zealand Red Stag, but it doesn't come easy. Dense fog, steep terrain, and thick vegetation make finding a mature stag no easy task. Watch as Mark Peterson and his guide cover the gorgeous New Zealand landscape in search of one of the most sought-after big game animals on the South Island of New Zealand. On this hunt, Mark was unable to get his firearms import permit in time to take his Gunwerks rifle, so he'd again have to borrow from his guide.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark Peterson is in Ethiopia hunting Mountain Nyala. After the first two days, Mark has taken a Menelik’s Bushbuck and a Colobus Monkey but no Mountain Nyala. Finally, on Day 3 the hunting party receives the green light from the military to hunt in the area they wanted. It's time to put the Gunwerks rifle to the test.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Continuing on with his quest for The North America Deer Slam, Mark Peterson packs his Gunwerks Nexus 300 PRC rifle for a hunt in Mexico targeting the elusive Mexican Texanus Whitetail from elevated blinds. The outfitter here has done an amazing job managing the deer numbers and age groups to grow giant whitetails. Mark is anticipating the pre-rut will get these big bucks moving.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Join Mark Peterson as he embarks on a thrilling hunt in Croatia for the Eurasian Brown Bear. Experience the beauty of the Croatian wilderness and witness the skill and precision required for a successful hunt. Follow along as Mark faces all the challenges dealing with wind and a big bear that is extremely smart, making it very difficult to harvest this amazing animal. Don’t miss this exciting hunting adventure with Mark Peterson in Croatia!

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Follow along as Mark Peterson travels to Tanzania to hunt the Eastern and Western White-Bearded Wildebeest. These animals are known for their distinctive white beards and large horns, making them a sought-after trophy for hunters on an African safari. Mark sets out on an exciting and thrilling adventure to track down these elusive creatures in the plains of Tanzania.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Join Mark Peterson as he heads to Kentucky for the late muzzleloader season. Already having an unforgettable season so far after taking “Freak Show” back in early September, he is looking to cap off his Kentucky season with a great buck with his Muzzleloader.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: Hunting Wisconsin gobblers into the final weeks of May comes with its challenges. Birds have faced a full season of hunting pressure, and they’re always a moment away from flipping the switch into summer patterns. But those last few eager longbeards make it worth the grind. In this episode of the Turkey POSSE, Grant Hudgens tags his first ever out-of-state strutter and becomes a card-carrying member of the Turkey POSSE.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: Turkey hunting in Texas hits differently — especially when it’s your first time behind the gun. In this week’s HuntStand TURKEY POSSE episode, Will Cooper introduces longtime best friends Aubry Gold and Chance Eckert to the world of spring turkey hunting in the Lone Star State. What starts as a casual hunt quickly turns into full-blown addiction as Rio Grande gobblers fire up, decoys get worked, and the Texas cedars come alive.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: After a long winter, Pete Osterman is eager to knock down his Minnesota gobbler with Josh Dahlke for their annual opening-week tradition. But like many turkey hunts, the POSSE gets the runaround at fly-down and end up grinding all day for an opportunity.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: It’s D Season in Wisconsin. After a well-scouted plan falls apart, Josh Dahlke’s solo hunt leads him to a new permission property that he’s never set foot on. E-scouting pays off and a late morning gobbler commits to seductive calling paired with an Avian-X decoy.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: After a long winter, Pete Osterman is eager to knock down his Minnesota gobbler with Josh Dahlke for their annual opening-week tradition. But like many turkey hunts, the POSSE gets the runaround at fly-down and end up grinding all day for an opportunity.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: Josh Dahlke makes the long-awaited journey to Hawaii as a critical piece to the Super Slam puzzle. Hawaii offers a turkey hunting adventure like none other, and there’s nobody resident companion than Jon Sabati – a Hawaii turkey hunting legend.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: Will Cooper flies from sunny Texas to chilly Minnesota for his first out-of-state turkey hunt. After an ice-cold morning in the turkey woods, the temps begin to rise and the POSSE regroups to find a midday gobbler that’s willing to work.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.desc: The TURKEY POSSE is on a three-state turkey hunting quest in the Northeast. Josh Dahlke stops in Maine and goes after a sneaky river-bottom gobbler.

programme.title: Dirt
programme.desc: Episode one hits close to home. Specifically, Josh’s hometown of Seattle where we travel from the mountains to the coast in pursuit of local ingredients for an authentic Washington meal. Digging for forest delicacies, hunting for exotic bivalves, and exploring the tidal flats of a multi-generational shellfish farm—it’s all on the menu.

programme.title: Dirt
programme.desc: In Episode 2: New Orleans, Josh links up with Mason Hereford (owner of award-winning sandwich shop Turkey and the Wolf) and then hits the road for some epic hog hunting, swamp fishing, crawfish and oyster harvesting, and exploring Louisiana’s only water buffalo dairy farm. It all culminates in one incredible feast featuring unique regional dishes prepared by Mason with the help of some local friends.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Captain Scott Walker fishes out of Hawks Cay with fellow Duck Key resident Captain Ariel Medero catching Blackfin Tunas and big Queen Snappers.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger chase the full moon Wahoo bite fishing out of Plantation Boat Mart.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Captain Scott Walker heads swordfishing out of Hawks Cay with his friend, retired WWE wrestler Travis Tomko.

programme.title: Life On The Water
programme.desc: Captains Scott Walker and Ariel Medero fish with David Moss from the Nature Conservancy out of Hawks Cay sampling the bottom fishing and deep dropping. In between the action they learn about the Nature Conservancy’s mission to promote sustainable and transparent fishing practices.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger break in Plantation Boat Mart’s new 36’ Invincible with some muttons for dinner and an epic shallow water sailfish double.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger fish with Daiwa’s Marc Mills out of Plantation Boat Mart trying out all of Daiwa’s new jigging equipment.

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: Capt. Jared and Capt. Jake Leon hop in the bay boat in search of a back country slam.

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: Capt. Jared Raskob joins his long time friend Capt. Jake Roth for a day of fishing in the Florida Keys.

programme.title: The Direction
programme.desc: Mark takes you to his home lake in Taylorsville, KY – Taylorsville Lake for some fall bassin’. Mark normally has a guest but with this being his final network filmed show he takes us us on a journey of telling how he got started and what he’s getting into.Mark’s outdoor career spurred from Taylorsville Lake and the show will wrap up there. You’ll also get a view from sponsors, guest and producers of the show of what its meant to them. And what an amazing fall day of bassin’.

programme.title: LBP Spotlight
programme.desc: Josh and his wife Anna own the most prolific tackle shop in Fort Walton Beach. Josh's family eats, sleeps and breathes fishing.

programme.title: Operation Healing Heroes
programme.desc: Motorcycle junkie and Army veteran Dave Allen recounts the terrifying prospect of facing chemical warfare during Operation Desert Storm with host Jay Garstecki and special guest Paul Sr. from American Choppers.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Trenton makes a trip to Louisiana to fish with Theophile Bourgeois at the Cajun Vista Lodge. With an early rain delay, they took a tour of the lodge and learned about its long standing family history before hitting the water for some redfish.

programme.title: Saltwater Underground
programme.desc: Nick visits Key Largo to search out backwater battles with snook, reds, trout and surprises in Florida Bay with Capt. Bruce Davis.

programme.title: Local Lines
programme.desc: Capt. Brandon Branch, Steve Grant and Kelly Player leave Jacksonville, FL for the Gulfstream to get into some Mahi.

programme.title: Beyond The Surface
programme.desc: In this episode, Capt. Danny joins his good buddy Capt. Brandon Branch in Crystal River. The two are in search of the areas legendary and hard fighting shallow-water Gag Grouper!

programme.title: Baitfuel Fishing Tournament
programme.desc: The BaitFuel Fishing Tour kicks off it’s season in Quinte West, Ontario where the teams will fish this Largemouth Bass Only event on the water of the Bay of Quinte.

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: Capt. Jared joins his best new bud, Capt. Garrett George for a summertime Mahi bash.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In part 1 of the British episodes, Andy starts his road trip around the United Kingdom in the Cotswolds where he sets out to hunt and taste the beautiful venison that Michelin star chef, Mark Robinson has to offer. He then proceeds to travel to the border of Scotland where he samples some of the finest and most historic bird shooting practices found throughout the British Isles.

programme.title: Super Retriever
programme.desc: The Super Retriever Series Crown Championship brings together the nation’s top canine athletes and handlers in Shreveport-Bossier City, Louisiana. In Part 1 of the Amateur Division, teams face off in challenging Retriever Trials alongside high-energy SRS Super Dock competition. Precision, power, and teamwork are put to the test as the road to the Crown begins.

programme.title: Super Retriever
programme.desc: The Super Retriever Series Crown Championship brings together the nation’s top canine athletes and handlers in Shreveport-Bossier City, Louisiana. In Part 1 of the Amateur Division, teams face off in challenging Retriever Trials alongside high-energy SRS Super Dock competition. Precision, power, and teamwork are put to the test as the road to the Crown begins.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: David Blanton and Mike Chamberlin have 2 incredible late season hunts in South Dakota. A flock comes into the hen decoy and a Jake trying to breed the decoy drives the Toms insane!

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: It's the next Grand Slam stop. The Spring Thunder crew hunts eastern wild turkeys in Georgia. They get there just in the nick of time. When they arrive at producer James Thompsons' farm, a screaming bird is waiting for them.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Realtree’s Anthony Virga takes us to his home turf in New Jersey and shows us two epic turkey hunts back-to-back!

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Spring Thunder's Phillip Culpepper turkey hunts Wisconsin for late-season gobblers with United Country's Travis Hamele. They have an incredible hunt, and turkeys fall from the skies.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: David Blanton and Mike Chamberlin head west for an unforgettable Merriam turkey hunt in the Black Hills of Wyoming and Montana. From breathtaking western landscapes to heart-pounding close encounters, the Merriams put on a show as the team works the birds across big country.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Realtree's David Blanton talks about some of his essential turkey hunting gear. These are the items that he has found to be the handiest throughout the years.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Tyler Jordan heads back to his college stomping grounds at Ole Miss to hunt with Athletic Director Keith Carter and William Jones. After a tough few days they were finally able to find some birds that were willing to work!

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Spring Thunder continues to roll across New York State turkey hunting public land gobblers!! After using OnX to find a large tract of public land along the shores of an Adirondack Mountain lake, the guys load up the boat to try and strike up a gobbling turkey.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: We are blessed today to hunt with Cole Dutton for the Inaugural Pierce Dutton Memorial Hunt. We are honored to spend a weekend with Cole and his family at the G2 Ranch. Disabled Outdoorsman has played a huge part in getting those less fortunate outdoors. We are truly blessed to be able to follow along and capture these great moments.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: In 2010, Amberly Snyder’s life changed forever when an accident left her paralyzed from the waist-down. Her comeback story not unlike something you’d see in a movie…in fact it inspired one. Disabled Outdoorsmen USA contacted TTHA and set up this hunt and we were fortunate enough to be able to capture just some of Amberly’s story and her harvest of a beautiful Axis buck at the G2 Ranch in the spring of 2022.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: We follow along with the crew from Struttinbuck on their annual Father Son hunt at FE Hill. No matter what happens throughout the year this is always a must have trip and a time to settle down and enjoy the great outdoors.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: This is the story of how FE Hill ranch got it's start. From its inception, FE Hill has created opportunities for people to experience the outdoors and create memories. For us, it has created a tradition of Fathers and Sons hunting together.

programme.title: Dirt
programme.desc: They say you come to New Zealand as a stranger, but leave as family. In this episode of DIRT, the crew goes full circle—meeting Muttonbird chefs Emma and George on the South Island before hopping up to Raglan and working their way back down. Water skiing next to snow-capped mountains, cooking in hot springs the Māori way, and stargazing at Wairarapa Dark Sky Reserve are all on the itinerary. But it’s the final “Feed” done Kiwi-style that makes one thing clear—in this unique and stunning land, nobody’s a stranger for long.

programme.title: Rocky Mountain Elk Foundation
programme.desc: “Part of our responsibility as citizens in this country is to make sure we not only take care of these resources but we hand it off to the next generation in better condition that we found it.” -Bob Ziehmer, Senior Director of Conservation for Bass Pro Shops & Cabela's Join Bob in New Mexico as he talks about taking care of our natural resources for future generations. Special thanks to Express UU Bar Ranch

programme.title: American Heroes Outdoors
programme.desc: On this episode, we travel north of the border for an unforgettable PART 1 Canadian fly-in fishing trip with Mandatory Fun Outdoors. We also highlight our next Scheels Hometown Hero, Jay Strande!

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: It’s still opening week of bow season in Oklahoma and the crew has three tags left to fill.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Bow season is finally here and the boys are at the Dirty Bird in Oklahoma. Randy is chasing a buck he named "The Camp Buck" because of how close the buck is to camp. If Randy can get it done this will be his biggest buck to date in Oklahoma.

programme.title: The Green Way Outdoors
programme.desc: Kyle and Jeff wade into the flooded timber of the legendary Coca-Cola Woods, where mallards drop through the treetops in waves. Amidst echoes of calling and wingbeats, they experience one of America’s most iconic duck hunts.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In part 2 of the British episodes, Andy visits the majestic land of Scotland. Here he is introduced to Scottish deer stalking, a type of hunting that is full of history and etiquette. He hunts ‘monarch’ stags and goes into the Atlantic waters to find some of the freshest shellfish on the planet which he, and Mike Robinson cook and eat. Concluding the British episodes in a place as wild as Scotland really sets the tone for the series.

programme.desc: During this episode of Ship Shape TV Essentials the SSTV team is at John’s lake house on Tupper Lake, NY. They are checking out some of the damage done to John’s pontoon decking from freezing weather they received. John and the team are upgrading the material, and adding carpeting to the pontoon decking. We also get some information on SPX-Johnson bilge pumps and the ease of access they provide.

programme.desc: David Blanton has a gobbler in his lap right off the Roost in Kansas hunting with JB Outfitters.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: It's 2016 and Mark heads down to Kansas for an early season Muzzleloader Whitetail hunt with Salt River Outfitters. It was hot in the stand with little deer movement until the moon phase switched and one of the bucks Mark had been looking for stepped out. Kansas is one of those states that has an early Muzzleloader season that opens in September, and if you can get the weather and the moon to align you can catch a big deer on his feet in daylight.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark Peterson's next adventure takes him west to Nevada for a giant bull elk with an early rifle elk tag in his pocket. As a non-resident, Mark had less than a 1% chance to draw this tag with Worldwide Trophy Adventures (WTA) TAGS Program. This is why he loves the tag system, you never know when you're going to get a tag. This area provides one of the best elk tags in the state of Nevada. Mark is bringing his brand new Gunwerks Nexus 300 PRC rifle to the high mountains of the Nevada desert.

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: PCB Fishing report author Nathan Chennaux joins Capt. Justin scanning the beach waters for tarpon and hooks into a monster while maneuvering around storms and vacationers. Does it get to the boat?

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Charles Rodney’s relationship with his hunting dogs, passion for field-to-table living and connection to the land make his love for chasing rabbits contagious while the heritage of Winchester’s small game ammunition makes this beloved pastime accessible for generations to come. Also: a Model 42 collection for the ages and hunting Kansas coyotes with the Keefer Brothers.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Deer season — the sights, the sounds and the smells stick with you. It’s about remembering those who came before us and setting the right example for those who look up to us. It’s about protecting the tradition, so they always have something to look forward to. When communities come together, we see the real reason for this special time of year.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Nobody chases the waterfowl migration as hard as Shawn Stahl of Team Winchester. The Michigan native spends over half of his year running the highways and flyways searching for the x. In this episode, we’ll tag along with Shawn and his crew with their Winchester pumps in hand as they hunker down hiding in fields and timber in hopes of locked wings from ducks and geese.

programme.title: Mortal
programme.desc: A psychologist finds herself drawn to a mysterious murder suspect who claims to possess godlike powers.

programme.title: Private Benjamin
programme.desc: A widow finds herself in the Women's Army Corps.

programme.desc: A drunken playboy decides to give up his inheritance for true love.

programme.desc: A quartet of eerie tales pay homage to Rod Serling's landmark TV series.

programme.title: Cannonball Run II
programme.desc: The usual suspects return for a new breakneck, ocean-to-ocean free for all!

programme.title: Message in a Bottle
programme.desc: A lonely woman's discovery of a man's letter in a bottle leads to love.

programme.title: Hooper
programme.desc: An action-packed comedy about a stuntman who wants to go out with a bang.

programme.title: Conspiracy Theory
programme.desc: A taxi driver reveals his conspiracy theories to the justice department.

programme.title: The Island Of Dr. Moreau
programme.desc: A deranged scientist performs genetic experiments with horrific results.

programme.title: Deal of the Century
programme.desc: An arms dealer attempts to sell war planes to a South American dictator.

programme.title: Lady in the Water
programme.desc: An apartment superintendent discovers a creature in the complex's pool.

programme.title: National Lampoon's Senior Trip
programme.desc: High schoolers head to D.C. after the President responds to their letter.

programme.title: Tenacious D in The Pick of Destiny
programme.desc: Musicians JB and KG set out on a quest for a legendary guitar pick.

programme.title: Death to Smoochy
programme.desc: Embittered ex-TV host of a children’s show tries to kill off his successor.

programme.title: Nothing but Trouble
programme.desc: A couple's trip takes a detour to a mansion filled with contraptions.

programme.title: Corrina, Corrina
programme.desc: A family devastated by grief meets a woman who helps heal the loss.

programme.title: Club Paradise
programme.desc: A Chicago fireman holds onto his dream of owning a tropical island resort.

programme.title: Mars Attacks!
programme.desc: Martians delight in incinerating humans in Tim Burton's sci-fi send-up.

programme.title: The Accidental Tourist
programme.desc: A travel writer hates to travel and loves order, until everything changes.

programme.title: Beyond the Poseidon Adventure
programme.desc: The S.S. Poseidon lies belly-up while the passengers fight to survive.

programme.title: Funny Farm (1988)
programme.desc: A city slicker moves his family to the country with disastrous results.

programme.title: Caddyshack II
programme.desc: The snobs of Bushwood Country Club don't know who they're messing with.

programme.title: Darkness Falls
programme.desc: A troubled man returns home to save his childhood sweetheart and her little brother from the evil spirit that murdered his mother.

programme.title: ATF
programme.desc: The Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco and Firearms pieces together crime scenes.

programme.title: Kabluey
programme.desc: To keep his brother’s family together during his deployment in Iraq, slacker Uncle Salman takes a humiliating job as a giant corporate mascot.

programme.title: Perfect
programme.desc: An intrepid Rolling Stone reporter bumps up against a publicity-shy aerobics instructor while covering a sexy scoop on her Los Angeles health club.

programme.title: Punchline
programme.desc: Steve, a broke stand-up comedian, develops a friendship and mentorship with Lilah, a homemaker with an ambition to be a stand-up comedian.

programme.title: Nothing in Common
programme.desc: A hilarious comedy about a perpetual adolescent who's forced to deal with his aging, unloving father when his mother leaves him flat.

programme.title: Chances Are
programme.desc: A man's love for his pregnant wife, Corinne Jeffries (Cybill Shepherd), is interrupted when a car accident sends him to heaven. He is reincarnated, however, and two decades later he is a writer named Alex Finch (Robert Downey Jr.). But when Alex starts dating Miranda Jeffries (Mary Stuart Masterson) -- his all-grown-up daughter from his previous life -- he remembers his love for Corinne. This spells trouble for his past-life best friend, Philip Train (Ryan O'Neal), who is now pursuing Corinne.

programme.title: The Throwaways
programme.desc: After a shrewd hacker is captured by the CIA, he agrees to work with them as long as he gets to pick the members of his ragtag team.

programme.title: U-Turn
programme.desc: A two-bit crook on his way to Vegas to pay a gambling debt to the Russian mob meets a couple who hope to use him to settle their personal vendettas.

programme.title: Enough
programme.desc: A woman's whirlwind romance sours when she discovers her husband's abusive nature, forcing her to escape with her young daughter and fight back.

programme.title: Money Monster
programme.desc: A financial TV host and his producer are put in an extreme situation when an irate investor who has lost everything forcibly takes over their studio.

programme.title: Maximum Risk
programme.desc: A police officer and his twin brother's fiancée team up to expose an international conspiracy in this erotic, globe-hopping adventure.

programme.title: The Lazarus Project
programme.desc: A reformed thief is drawn back into a burglary-gone-wrong that lands him on death row, until he mysteriously wakes up in a psychiatric hospital.

programme.title: Dragon Wars
programme.desc: Once in 500 years, ancient mythical creatures come to Earth, wreaking havoc and destruction. This time they must be stopped for good.

programme.title: Anaconda 3: Offspring
programme.desc: With giant, genetically engineered snakes on the loose and reproducing, a mercenary and his team must race to stop the serpents’ destructive spree.

programme.title: Final Descent
programme.desc: When his commercial aircraft collides with another plane in midair, it's up to Captain Glen 'Lucky' Singer to save the lives of his 319 passengers.

programme.title: Final Run
programme.desc: On a family trip, Glen "Lucky" Singer must stop a train from careening toward disaster when an engineer's mistake causes a glitch in the controls.

programme.title: Little Nikita
programme.desc: Roy Parmenter (Sidney Poitier) is an FBI agent with a long-standing vendetta against a Russian spy named Scuba (Richard Lynch). Now, Scuba is blackmailing the Soviet government by tracking down and assassinating their sleeper agents in the United States. Parmenter discovers that high-school student Jeff Grant (River Phoenix) is the son of two such agents (Caroline Kava, Richard Jenkins), which is a total shock to Jeff. Parmenter must try to track Scuba while also protecting the Grants.

programme.title: The Juror
programme.desc: With his gangster boss (Tony Lo Bianco) on trial for murder, a mob thug known as "the Teacher" (Alec Baldwin) tells Annie Laird (Demi Moore) she must talk her fellow jurors into a not-guilty verdict, implying that he'll kill her son Oliver (Joseph Gordon-Levitt) if she fails. She manages to do this, but, when it becomes clear that the mobsters might want to silence her for good, she sends Oliver abroad and tries to gather evidence of the plot against her, setting up a final showdown.

programme.title: 30 Minutes or Less
programme.desc: A small-town pizza delivery guy’s dull routine collides with the wild ambitions of two bumbling crooks scheming an impossible bank heist.

programme.title: The Replacement Killers
programme.desc: Hired assassin John Lee (Chow Yun-Fat) is asked by Chinatown crime boss Terence Wei (Kenneth Tsang) to murder the young son of policeman Stan Zedkov (Michael Rooker). Lee has the boy in his sights, but his conscience gets the better of him, and he spares the child's life. Afraid that Wei will take revenge on his family in China, Lee seeks out expert forger Meg Coburn (Mira Sorvino) to obtain the passport he needs to get out of the country, but a band of replacement killers is soon on his trail.

programme.title: Memoirs of a Geisha
programme.desc: Based on the novel by Arthur Golden.

programme.title: The Jolson Story
programme.desc: At the turn of the 20th century, young Asa Yoelson (Scotty Beckett) decides to go against the wishes of his cantor father (Ludwig Donath) and pursue a career in show business. Gradually working his way up through the vaudeville ranks, Asa -- now calling himself Al Jolson (Larry Parks) -- joins a blackface minstrel troupe and soon builds a reputation as a consummate performer. But as his career grows in size, so does his ego, resulting in battles in business as well as in his personal life.

programme.title: Sahara
programme.desc: A stranded group of six Allied soldiers trek across the desert, making their way to an abandoned fort, but are soon faced with a German battalion.

programme.title: Fire Down Below
programme.desc: The long-standing bond between two small-time smugglers sailing around the Caribbean is threatened when a gorgeous siren seeks passage on their boat.

programme.title: Born Free
programme.desc: A British couple in Kenya that raises an orphaned lion cub to adulthood train her to live in the wild when a warden demands she be sent to a zoo.

programme.title: Mickey One
programme.desc: A stand-up comic with gambling debts runs away to Chicago to escape the mob, but his yearning to get back on stage may compromise his new identity.

programme.title: Gidget Grows Up
programme.desc: Heartbroken after her boyfriend joins the Air Force without a word, Gidget seeks peace by becoming a tour guide at the United Nations in New York.

programme.title: Craig's Wife
programme.desc: A pushy pessimist with an eye for the finer things in life, Harriet (Rosalind Russell) has married Walter Craig (John Boles) because he is able to provide the kind of posh lifestyle she is certain she deserves. She feels not the slightest bit of guilt about her lack of affection for her husband, and aside from John himself, everyone around her seems to realize this. But when her husband gets a scare involving the police, Harriet's way of life is threatened.

programme.title: Funny Girl
programme.desc: In this bittersweet, classic musical drama, the vibrant and beautiful young Fanny Brice (Barbra Streisand) starts out as a bit player on the New York City vaudeville stage, but works her way up to stardom on Broadway. Valued for her vocal and comedic talents by the renowned theater impresario Florenz Ziegfeld (Walter Pidgeon), Fanny thrives, but her relationship with her suave, imprisoned businessman husband, Nick Arnstein (Omar Sharif), is another story.

programme.title: Bye Bye Birdie
programme.desc: When the draft selects rock star Conrad Birdie, his fans are devastated, but none more than struggling songwriter Albert Peterson (Dick Van Dyke), whose song Birdie was just about to record. Albert's longtime girlfriend, Rosie (Janet Leigh), pushes Albert to write a new tune that Birdie will perform on television to a fan selected in a contest. The scheme works, with young Ohio teenager Kim McAfee (Ann-Margret) declared the winner, but no one has counted on the jealous wrath of her boyfriend.

programme.title: The Big Heat
programme.desc: A police officer seems to have committed suicide, but Detective Dave Bannion (Glenn Ford) thinks there's more to the story. After talking to the man's mob-connected mistress, Bannion discovers far-reaching corruption that leads all the way from the police station to kingpin Mike Lagana (Alexander Scourby). But when the policeman's lover is killed, it becomes clear that crossing Lagana is a dangerous thing to do. Soon Bannion finds himself marked for death and his family in mortal danger.

programme.title: The Three Stooges in Orbit
programme.desc: When a pair of Martians steal a powerful submarine-tank-rocket military weapon from an inventor, the Three Stooges step in to try to save the day.

programme.title: First Comes Courage
programme.desc: Beautiful Norwegian woman Nicole Larsen (Merle Oberon) is dedicated to the local resistance movement, and seduces Nazi officer Paul Dichter (Carl Esmond) in order to gain information that will help to defeat the Germans. Complicating matters for Nicole are both the difficulty of her loathsome undercover work and the appearance of an old flame, handsome English soldier Allan Lowell (Brian Aherne), who would like nothing more than to reunite with her.

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: This steamy pot-boiler follows the intrigues surrounding an escaped convict who attempts to return to the small southern town where he grew up.

programme.title: Miss Grant Takes Richmond
programme.desc: A bookie operating behind a real estate front gets pulled into the business for real when he hires a daffy secretary who thinks the office is legit.

programme.title: Jason and the Argonauts
programme.desc: A fearless sailor returns to the kingdom of Thessaly after a 20-year voyage to find the Golden Fleece and make his rightful claim to the throne.

programme.title: The Doctor Takes a Wife
programme.desc: A writer (Loretta Young) and a neurologist (Ray Milland) accept rumors of their marriage when each stands to gain from the mistake.

programme.title: Murderer's Row
programme.desc: Secret agent Matt Helm must rescue the kidnapped inventor of an ultra-destructive "helio beam" capable of destroying Washington, D.C.

programme.title: It Happened One Night
programme.desc: When a brash reporter starts trailing a runaway heiress on her bus trip from Florida to New York for a big scoop, can love be far behind?

programme.title: Guess Who's Coming to Dinner
programme.desc: A daughter visits her family to introduce them to her Black fiancé, whose kindness and loving demeanor changes the hearts of her reluctant parents.

programme.title: Easy Rider
programme.desc: Two hippie bikers embark on a journey to discover "the real America" and take the ultimate bad trip in this landmark American film.

programme.title: Count Three and Pray
programme.desc: After fighting for the Union during the Civil War, Luke Fargo (Van Heflin) is greeted with resentment when he returns home to his small southern town. Luke has a reputation as a rogue, but he's reinvented himself as a man of God and plans to fix up the town's damaged church. Recalling their romantic past, Georgina Decrais (Allison Hayes) tries to reclaim Luke. But, to the dismay of some suspicious townspeople, he concentrates on mentoring an unsophisticated young woman, Lissy (Joanne Woodward).

programme.title: The Parson and the Outlaw
programme.desc: Billy the Kid (Anthony Dexter) retires from the outlaw life, fakes his own death and adopts the name William Bonney. Soon, William runs into Jack Slade (Sonny Tufts), a gun-toting stranger who is itching for a gunfight. Jack falls in with a group of thugs terrorizing William's town. The Rev. Jericho Jones (Buddy Rogers) exhorts William to defend the townspeople, and, when William's maid, Tonya (Marie Windsor), is beaten by the thugs, he grabs his pistol.

programme.title: The Prescott Kid
programme.desc: A cowboy finds himself in serious trouble with a notorious outlaw.

programme.title: Gunman's Walk
programme.desc: Rancher Lee Hackett (Van Heflin) spends so much time trying to keep the violent streak of his son Ed (Tab Hunter) in check, he neglects the needs of his other son Davy (James Darren). When Ed's temper results in the death of an innocent Native American, Lee tries his best to cover his son's tracks by making a deal with unscrupulous horse trader Jensen (Ray Teal), who falsely testifies on Ed's behalf. But it becomes clear that Ed can't change, and a shootout between father and son ensues.

programme.title: Blackjack Ketchum, Desperado
programme.desc: A gunfighter (Howard Duff) wants peace in the valley, but a cattle baron (Victor Jory) and his brothers want war.

programme.title: Traveling Saleswoman
programme.desc: A soapmaker's daughter (Joan Davis) goes to the Wild West, followed by her boyfriend (Andy Devine).

programme.title: Face of a Fugitive
programme.desc: While being escorted to jail, bank robber Jim Larson (Fred MacMurray) manages to get the upper hand on his captor and escape. Jim is surprised to see his brother appear on the scene, ready to aid his sibling. As they make their getaway, his brother and the pursuing lawman shoot each other, leaving the stunned Jim alone and on the run. Assuming a different identity, Jim travels to a mining town and befriends the local sheriff (Lin McCarthy), hoping to start over and find redemption.

programme.title: The Desperadoes
programme.desc: Sheriff Steve Upton (Randolph Scott) is a devoted lawman who works tirelessly to keep order in his small town, nestled within the Utah territory. So, when Cheyenne Rogers (Glenn Ford), an old friend with a criminal past, rolls into town, Upton is immediately on edge. Cheyenne assures his pal that all that's behind him now. But when the town bank is robbed, Cheyenne becomes the locals' number one suspect. He'll have to prove his innocence to save himself and his friend's reputation.

programme.title: The Return of Daniel Boone
programme.desc: The scout's grandson (Bill Elliott) foils land-grabbers; his sidekick (Dub Taylor) flirts with twins.

programme.title: Stranger From Arizona
programme.desc: A quiet cowboy (Buck Jones) on a white horse saves a gal (Dorothy Fay) from cattle rustlers.

programme.title: Last of the Comanches
programme.desc: A cavalry sergeant (Broderick Crawford) leads soldiers, civilians and an Indian (Johnny Stewart) on a desert trek.

programme.title: Both Barrels Blazing
programme.desc: The Durango Kid (Charles Starrett) shoots holes in a scheme to ship stolen gold.

programme.title: The Violent Men
programme.desc: Former Union soldier John Parrish (Glenn Ford) wants to sell his ranch and start a new life by settling down out east. However, the ruthless tactics of Lew Wilkison (Edward G. Robinson), the scheming businessman interested in his property, make Parrish reconsider the move, especially after one of his ranch hands is murdered. Adding to the increasingly tense conflict is Wilkison's wife, Martha (Barbara Stanwyck), who is cheating on her husband with his brother, Cole (Brian Keith).

programme.title: The Texican
programme.desc: Deputy Jess Carlin (Audie Murphy) leaves his Texas hometown in disgrace, framed by corrupt saloon owner Luke Starr (Broderick Crawford) for a crime he didn't commit. When his brother, muckraking newspaper editor Roy Carlin (Víctor Vilanova), is murdered after printing an exposé of Starr's crooked deals, Carlin returns home from the Mexican border town where he's been holed up and enlists his brother's fiancée (Luz Márquez) and saloon dancer Kit O'Neal (Diana Lorys) to bring Starr down.

programme.title: Unknown Valley
programme.desc: Joe goes looking for his missing father in the desert, then strange villagers take him captive; he finds his father and plans to escape, taking with him a woman that he has fallen for.

programme.title: The Westerner
programme.desc: A rancher (Tim McCoy) and his buddy (Joseph Sauers) scare rustlers out of business.

programme.title: The Texas Rangers
programme.desc: The Civil War has ended, the Union Army has pulled out, and now the Texas Rangers are needed once again to protect their state from harm. The newly reformed Rangers decide to take a chance by hiring a notorious outlaw Johnny Carver (George Montgomery). They offer the desperado amnesty for his previous crimes if he'll put his famous trigger finger to work for Texas. Carver agrees, but his new job won't be easy, as his old rival, the Sundance Kid (Ian MacDonald), is out for vengeance.

programme.title: Santa Fe
programme.desc: Three brothers become outlaws after the Civil War, while a fourth sibling goes to work for the railroad.

programme.title: The Mountain Men
programme.desc: Two old-time Wyoming fur trappers (Charlton Heston, Brian Keith) try to protect a runaway Indian (Victoria Racimo) from her angry ex-mate.

programme.title: The California Trail
programme.desc: Cowboy Santa Fe Stewart (Buck Jones) fights a tyrant in Spanish California.

programme.title: Comanche Station
programme.desc: When solitary cowboy Jefferson Cody (Randolph Scott) hears tales of a white woman living as a captive of the Comanche tribe, he rides deep into the tribe's territory to exchange goods for her freedom. As Cody escorts the captive woman away, he learns that she is Nancy Lowe (Nancy Gates), and that there is a sizable reward offered by her husband for her return. Cody and Mrs. Lowe must brave ruthless bounty hunters greedy for the reward, as well as the now-warring Comanches, to make it to safety.

programme.title: Land Raiders
programme.desc: An Arizona land-grabber (Telly Savalas) and his brother (George Maharis) renew their feud amid Army troops and Apaches.

programme.title: Sierra Stranger
programme.desc: Jess Collins, a poor miner, helps Sonny chase off two men he calls claim jumpers. When he arrives in the nearby town, he learns Sonny is no good and everyone in town is against him.

programme.title: Phantom Stagecoach
programme.desc: A dispute between stage lines ends with a steel-armored stagecoach.

programme.title: The Taming of the West
programme.desc: A new lawman (Bill Elliott) punches out cattle rustlers who like to shoot sheriffs.

programme.title: 40 Guns to Apache Pass
programme.desc: A cavalry captain (Audie Murphy) rescues settlers and catches a corporal (Kenneth Tobey) selling rifles to Apaches.

programme.title: The Man From Laramie
programme.desc: Will Lockhart (James Stewart) comes to the ranch town of Coronado looking for the man who is selling rifles to the Apaches, because his brother was killed with one. There he runs into Alec Waggoman (Donald Crisp), an aging rancher with a violent son named Dave (Alex Nicol). A conflict between Dave and Vic (Arthur Kennedy), Waggoman's top aid, helps Lockhart uncover the source of the rifles -- and also gets him involved in the power struggles at the Waggoman ranch.

programme.title: Grand Hotel
programme.desc: Guests at a posh Berlin hotel struggle through scandal and heartache.

programme.title: The Super Cops
programme.desc: Two rogue cops enlist a streetwalker to help them stop a deadly drug ring.

programme.title: The Man Who Would Be King
programme.desc: Two English ex-soldiers leave India to become kings in Afghanistan.

programme.title: What Ever Happened to Baby Jane?
programme.desc: Two fading actress-sisters whose relationship takes a dangerous turn.

programme.title: North by Northwest
programme.desc: Cary Grant plays a businessman caught in a web of intrigue.

programme.title: The Philadelphia Story
programme.desc: A socialite's first husband shows up on the eve of her second marriage.

programme.title: The Music Man
programme.desc: A con man falls in love and stays when he should take the money and leave.

programme.title: The Americanization of Emily
programme.desc: A British war widow falls for an American sailor during World War II.

programme.title: Please Don't Eat the Daisies
programme.desc: A drama critic and his family try to adjust to life in the country.

programme.title: Strangers on a Train
programme.desc: Two strangers strike a deadly pact in this Hitchcock classic.

programme.title: Ice Station Zebra
programme.desc: A U.S. submarine sails under the ice cap in search of a downed satellite.

programme.title: Wild Rovers
programme.desc: An aging cowboy joins up with a beginner to rob banks.

programme.title: Viva Las Vegas
programme.desc: A driver preparing for the Grand Prix whiles his time away in Las Vegas.

programme.title: Around the World In 80 Days
programme.desc: An adventurous Englishman wagers that he can circle the globe in 80 days.

programme.title: Mutiny on the Bounty
programme.desc: A first mate leads his crew in mutiny against their sadistic captain.

programme.title: National Velvet
programme.desc: A farm girl trains a difficult horse for the Grand National Steeplechase.

programme.title: Thirty Seconds Over Tokyo
programme.desc: Jimmy Doolittle trains troops for the first airborne attacks on Japan.

programme.title: The Roaring Twenties
programme.desc: Three war veteran friends start a bootlegging business in New York.

programme.title: The Listener
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a cooking show contestant, poisoned on live television.

programme.title: The Listener
programme.desc: While investigating the murder of a billionaire playboy, Toby's curiosities about his family arise.

programme.title: The Listener
programme.desc: After being drugged, Toby is implicated in an armed kidnapping.

programme.title: The Listener
programme.desc: Toby goes under cover when he believes an innocent man is taking the fall for a murder.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: When an 8-year-old is found dead on the Appalachian Trail, the team searches the forests for a man targeting children.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The team pursues a killer who targets women; Hotchner turns to an FBI cadet for help.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: After helping a man get parole, Morgan regrets his decision when the man murders someone shortly after being released.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: A series of bizarre, ritualistic murders brings the team to Miami; Reid experiences hallucinations and blinding headaches.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The team pursues two young lovers on a killing spree; Prentiss receives frightening news.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The team investigates unusual murders in Los Angeles, and more information about Prentiss' past arises.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The BAU heads to upstate New York to investigate mysterious disappearances; Prentiss receives troubling news about a former colleague.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: Reid's ability to connect with an autistic child may prove helpful in finding the boy's missing parents; two former colleagues help Prentiss search for an old adversary.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: After a series of murders, Prentiss becomes suspicious of a former nemesis (Sebastian Roché) and fears for the safety of her team.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: Prentiss prepares to confront her nemesis at last; the team asks J.J. to help find Prentiss and capture Doyle.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The team must stop a gang of suspected murderers that is targeting a new victim nightly.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The team must investigate targeted killings in Florida while each member is dealing with feelings about losing Prentiss.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: A stalker in San Diego targets college students; Strauss scrutinizes the team after the loss of Prentiss.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The team travels to a North Carolina town where one woman was found badly injured and another is missing, and discovers those may not be the only victims.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The team investigates when bodies are found buried beneath the ocean floor off Florida; Morgan is rattled when his aunt thinks her missing daughter is one of the victims.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The team investigates a suspected human trafficking ring; Hotchner suggests changes are coming; J.J. returns to the team.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: A Senate committee questions the members of the BAU team; a familiar face may help keep the team united.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: The BAU team investigates a series of murders in Oklahoma; Reid gives JJ and Prentiss the cold shoulder.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: After a mass murder at an Internet security company, evidence indicates that the person responsible is not a typical serial killer; Prentiss is required to complete recertification training.

programme.title: Criminal Minds
programme.desc: A killer targets survivors of a high-school massacre, who have gathered for a reunion; Hotch fears that his son is being bullied.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: MacGyver and the team must steal diamonds before terrorists beat them to it and trade the jewels for a weapon of mass destruction.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: When Murdoc drugs and kidnaps MacGyver, Mac must escape and join the team to save Murdoc's next victim.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: The team's Halloween plans are interrupted when the vice president's son's plane goes down with a mysterious prisoner on board.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: Mac and the team attempt to extinguish a massive oil wellhead fire set by a rebel group in Nigeria.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: MacGyver uses a heater and hot sauce to help a key presidential candidate in Ecuador receive a heart transplant he desperately needs after a rebel group tries to sabotage delivery of the organ.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: MacGyver and Jack "steal" a priceless painting to draw out an infamous stolen art dealer; Riley's estranged father asks to be part of her life; Matty sends Bozer to a spy training camp.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: Mac and the team follow mercenaries who are transporting stolen lethal nerve gas; Jack confronts Elwood about Riley; Bozer is pushed to his limit in spy school.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: Mac must help college students stranded on a research ship in the Arctic Ocean; Jack kidnaps Elwood to find out what he's up to; Bozer's feelings toward Leanna grow stronger.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: When the corpse of the host of a popular reality show is found partially decomposed in an outhouse, Brennan and Booth must separate the killer from many suspects; Booth discovers that Brennan juggles two relationships.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: When Booth's son, Parker, finds a finger in a bird's nest, the team launches an investigation to find the rest of the body; Booth asks Sweets to psychoanalyze his son; Sweets confronts Hodgins about his attitude in the lab.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: A teen discovers a dismembered, headless body in a pool of purple water; the team struggles to find enough evidence to locate a killer until getting insight from Zack, who studies the case at his psychiatric facility.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: Booth and Brennan investigate when the remains of a disliked office manager are found in a building elevator shaft; Angela and Hodgins try to figure out their altered relationship.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: Skeletal remains with remnants of breast implants are found in the Chesapeake Bay; when the body's lower half is discovered, the team realizes that initial assumptions about the victim may be wrong.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: Booth and Brennan investigate when the remains of a renowned artist are found in an impounded junkyard car; a woman connected to Angela becomes a suspect in the case.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: A con man misleads the team during a murder investigation; Booth's younger brother, Jared, takes a job at the Pentagon; Brennan agrees to go on a date with Jared to a White House function.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: While en route to China to identify prehistoric anthropological remains, Booth and Brennan are called upon to solve a murder in midair when the flight attendant discovers a fully cooked human body in the plane's microwave.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: Brennan and Booth investigate when a skull and other skeletal remains are found in a nationally protected wooded preserve; Brennan's recently acquitted father (Ryan O'Neal) finds a job as a teacher.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: A shallow grave holds the remains of conjoined twins; Booth and Brennan infiltrate a circus community after they discover that the twins were jugglers there.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby and DS Dan Scott investigate the shotgun murder of Jamie Cruikshank, a barman at the Maid in Splendour, a pub in the village of Midsomer Worthy.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When the curate of the local church in Midsomer Parva is burned alive in the effigy of the straw woman, DCI Tom Barnaby and DS Dan Scott find themselves investigating what must be a murder.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Exactly nine years after Ferdy Villers killed himself, his entire family reunites for Christmas unaware that someone is out for revenge.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The appearance of a headless horseman at Quitewell Hall foreshadows a number of deaths amongst an aristocratic family. In the midst of a Civil War re-enactment the family relive their own historic battles with their hostile neighbours. But what is the truth about the DeQuettevilles - and the ghostly rider?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a barrister is killed in Midsomer, suspicion falls on convicted murderer Grady Felton. Grady has just returned to the village where he committed the murder years ago amid much anger from locals. But Grady has a solid alibi. Barnaby suspects he is working with an accomplice - until Grady himself is targeted in an arson attack.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomer's own Stella Harris. When Stella's more famous sister returns after a 40-year family rift, the killings escalate, each of them reminiscent of a movie plot. Is life - and death - imitating art?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: As darkness covers Midsomer Stanton during a total eclipse of the sun, amateur astronomer Jeremy Harper is killed by a blow to the head with a meteorite. Barnaby and Jones soon discover intrigue, sexual tension and academic rivalry are rife among the star-seeking community.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Harriet Farmer wakes up after being in a coma since the night she tried to elope. Soon afterwards, a killer strikes members of a chess club. Could the attack which left Harriet for dead a year ago be linked to moves on a chess board? And where is her boyfriend Finn?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A woman is crushed to death with a giant round of cheese at the home of the world-famous Midsomer Blue. Secret and controversial plans to modernise the dairy are revealed while rumours about private lives of the parents cause a row at the prestigious local prep school. As other people linked to the dairy meet agonising deaths, long-held secrets start to emerge...

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A woman is killed after receiving threatening letters, setting off a chain of murders for DCI Barnaby to investigate.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: In the village of Midsomer Wellow, someone is killing off the bell ringers of the parish church, in the week before a big bell-ringing competition. One young woman on the team is even shot dead in the churchyard on her wedding day.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Some of the residents of Midsomer Worthy are trying to prevent James Harrington from turning Setwale Woods into a housing estate. Led by a solicitor, Bernadette Sullivan, and a local farmer, Simon Bartlett, who also happen to be having an affair, they haven't had much success in court. When Bartlett's wife Victoria is found dead in the woods, Barnaby and Troy must first determine is if her murder is related to the planned development or something as yet unknown. The information provided by two inquisitive children, who see the investigation as a fascinating game, proves invaluable in solving the crime.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An investigation of a car fire and a bludgeon murder lead Barnaby and Troy to investigate the members of a local reading club which has an agenda that goes beyond books.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby investigates the murder of a boy during a traditional annual race at an exclusive private school but meets with resentment.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: New DCI John Barnaby arrives in Midsomer and is bemused by the quaint villages and their quirky residents. But when a local DJ is crushed to death at a traditional girls' boarding school, he soon discovers that murder and deception are never far away. As the death toll rises, could Barnaby's first case also be his last?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The reclusive lives of elderly eccentrics William and Mary Bingham (Edward Fox and Phyllida Law) comes under police scrutiny when a social services investigator (Jeff Rawle) is murdered. Barnaby and Jones must unearth generations of family secrets and decipher astronomical charts to find the killer.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a young woman is dressed like a bride and drowned in a bath, it triggers a spate of ghoulish wedding-themed murders in Great Worthy. The case takes Barnaby and Jones to a donkey sanctuary, a heritage steam railway and a pub run by an ex-copper and former brothel madam. With the serial killer still at large, could history be repeating itself?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Jones goes undercover at the Oblong Foundation after one of the cult's young female members disappears suddenly. But he and Barnaby soon start digging into an old case involving the death of a couple in a boat explosion.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: In Midsomer Mow, the gruesome discovery of a farmer's eviscerated body in an ancient stone circle is made just days before the spring equinox.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A VAT inspector goes missing as he hunts for an illicit cider still responsible for brewing The Beast, a potent local hooch. At the Midsomer Abbas spring fayre which celebrates its friendship with Midsomer Herne, Barnaby and Jones sample the local cider, while temperance preacher Norman Grigor calls on residents to repent of their drunken ways. Suddenly, Barnaby is violently ill as the body of missing man Peter Slim is found floating in the cider vat. Meanwhile Samuel Quested (Warren Clarke), the traditionalist mayor of Midsomer Abbas, seeks to revive the ancient rite known as 'The Stag'.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the cloistered world of Midsomer Priory when a nun is strangled to death. The detectives uncover a complex mystery involving holy orders, teenage romantic liaisons, African art and missing antique silver as the community is forced to open its doors to 21st-century policing.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A row between keen birdwatchers in Midsomer-in-the-Marsh turns nasty when their president is killed. Patrick Morgan met his death while hoping to sight a rare bird, but is his obsession with ornithology to blame - or something more sinister?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Plans by Barnaby to renew his wedding vows at the church of St Michael's in Badger's Drift, are quickly put on hold when the body of Richard Bayly, a local developer, is found decapitated in his own home. The detective is left mystified as to why anyone would kill a man who had only been given weeks to live, after being recently diagnosed with a brain tumour. As he and Troy attempt to find out, they soon encounter a hotbed of corruption and property feuding occurring within the village as the body count rises and the mystery deepens.

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: Jane's fear of heights is tested when she and Davey investigate a skydiving accident.

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: Building work at an abandoned cottage unearths a child's hidden remains.

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: After a hoax callout, a volunteer in the Lighthaven lifeboat crew drowns.

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: A local journalist claims that there is a big cat on the moors, but nobody believes him.

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: Mick's rival landlord is found dead on the beach, and Jane must try to clear his name.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Grunge icon Scott Weiland died on Dec. 3, 2015, at the age of 48; his death came suddenly while on the road with his band The Wildabouts; Weiland, a singer and songwriter, was known as the last great rock star.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Christopher John Cornell was an American musician, singer and songwriter; he was best known as the lead vocalist for the rock bands Soundgarden and Audioslave; Cornell struggled with depression for much of his life.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Known as the founder of the Van Halen, a band synonymous with California cool, Eddie Van Halen sold multiple platinum albums and cemented himself in rock 'n' roll history; behind his contagious smile, there was a darker undercurrent.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: On Oct. 2, 2017, news of Tom Petty's sudden death at the age of 66 shocked the nation.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Prince, the pop superstar who sold over 100 million records over a 30 year career, was fit, health-conscious, and vehemently anti-drugs, and yet, on April 21, 2016, Prince died, shockingly, of a drug overdose; the case is still under investigation.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: At 9:50 a.m. on Aug. 16, 2018, Aretha Franklin tragically died; Aretha, one of the world's greatest vocalists, entertained audiences for over 50 years with her unique brand of gospel, soul and pop music.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Florence Ballard, a founding member of the popular Motown vocal female group the Supremes, leaves the group in 1967; she makes an attempt for a musical comeback but dies of a heart attack in February 1976 at the age of 32.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Daughter of Nat `King' Cole, Natalie Cole was a unique singing legend but in 2008 her dark past caught up with her and set off an extraordinary chain reaction of medical conditions.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: After nearly 40 years as the Queen of Disco and First Lady of Love, Donna Summer died from cancer at her home in Naples, Fla., on May 17, 2012, with the public having no idea she was ill.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Janis Joplin, a rock-and-roll icon whose explosive performances electrified audiences around the world, dies alone in her Los Angeles hotel on October 4, 1970, at the age of 27.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: The music world was stunned when outrageous entertainer and colorful funk star Rick James was found dead by his housekeeper; his excesses took a dark turn when in 1991, he was accused of kidnap and torture, resulting in a prison sentence.

programme.desc: Daughter of Nat `King' Cole, Natalie Cole was a unique singing legend but in 2008 her dark past caught up with her and set off an extraordinary chain reaction of medical conditions.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: After nearly 40 years as the Queen of Disco and First Lady of Love, Donna Summer died from cancer at her home in Naples, Fla., on May 17, 2012, with the public having no idea she was ill.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Janis Joplin, a rock-and-roll icon whose explosive performances electrified audiences around the world, dies alone in her Los Angeles hotel on October 4, 1970, at the age of 27.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: The music world was stunned when outrageous entertainer and colorful funk star Rick James was found dead by his housekeeper; his excesses took a dark turn when in 1991, he was accused of kidnap and torture, resulting in a prison sentence.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Grunge icon Scott Weiland died on Dec. 3, 2015, at the age of 48; his death came suddenly while on the road with his band The Wildabouts; Weiland, a singer and songwriter, was known as the last great rock star.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Christopher John Cornell was an American musician, singer and songwriter; he was best known as the lead vocalist for the rock bands Soundgarden and Audioslave; Cornell struggled with depression for much of his life.

programme.title: Autopsy: The Last Hours of...
programme.desc: Known as the founder of the Van Halen, a band synonymous with California cool, Eddie Van Halen sold multiple platinum albums and cemented himself in rock 'n' roll history; behind his contagious smile, there was a darker undercurrent.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Deborah Cox became a worldwide star in the late 90s when her breakthrough hit “Nobody’s Supposed To Be Here” took over the R&B charts for 14 weeks. Hailing from Toronto, Canada, she dominated the R&B and Dance charts with songs like “Sentimental”, “We Can’t Be Friends” and “Absolutely Not”.

programme.title: The Undershepard
programme.desc: A controversial tale of two ministers, LC and Roland who are best friends and brothers in Christ. LC�a mild-mannered man�is overcome by his access to power; money earned through his ministry, and slowly transforms into a ruthless businessman. STARS: ISAIAH WASHINGTON, LAMMAN RUCKER, ELISE NEAL, AND KEITH DAVID

programme.title: Uncensored
programme.desc: Going zero to 100 doesn’t always happen real quick. Success can be a long journey and according to most people, it’s worth the trouble. Take a look back at some of your favorite celebs like Tasha Smith, Kirk Franklin, Nick Cannon, and more as they describe their rags-to-riches story.

programme.title: Uncensored
programme.desc: Platinum selling R&B singer Keyshia Cole is known and loved for her transparency and authenticity. Through top selling albums and highly rated television shows, she has shared her life with the world. Now she's back with much more to say.

programme.title: Uncensored
programme.desc: Southern rapper Master P grows his "No Limit" brand to one of the most successful independent labels in the music industry, emerging a millionaire before the age of 30 thanks to his emphasis on ownership, generational wealth, and philanthropy.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Teddy Pendergrass,one of the most popular black recording artist in the world, and the first artist to achieve five consecutive multi-platinum albums, was an icon of romantic balladry.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: With one of the biggest, brassiest voices in R&B and contemporary jazz, along with a four-octave range, Vesta Williams charged through the 80s from an A-list backup singer, who recorded with the likes of Gladys Knight, Anita Baker, and Sting, to a hit-making diva. But Vesta�s surging stardom overwhelmed her, and efforts to comfort herself with drugs and food made matters worse. Though she continued to rely sporadically on pain-killers and sleep medication, she was determined to survive. In 2011, as she completed the definitive profile of her life for �Unsung�, Vesta Williams was back in high spirits. Then on September 22, 2011, she suddenly died in her sleep, at age 53.

programme.title: The Secret She Kept
programme.desc: Tia has just married the man of her dreams and is on the brink of launching a major political campaign when a pattern of erratic behavior leads her husband to uncover her secret: she suffers from mental illness.

programme.title: The Rickey Smiley Show
programme.desc: Sister Bernice protests at the radio station. Kenny takes a paternity test.

programme.title: Hollywood Divas
programme.desc: Paula is still on her lonely island after her blow up with Golden. Golden invites the ladies to be apart of her swimsuit fashion show. Malika wants to bring everyone together for her movie screening, but worries about Paula�s presence.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Whether singing, playing guitar, or crafting smooth-sailing hits like �Jack and Jill, �The Other Woman� or �You Can�t Change That�, Ray Parker Jr. made success look easy. But behind the show-biz fa�ade, Parker was an obsessive musician - a guitarist who�d cut his teeth with Motown�s house band, the Funk brothers, as a teenager, and later played with Stevie Wonder and Barry White. Long before his emergence as a headliner, he�d written hits for White and Chaka Khan, while crafting a Grammy winning single for Leo Sayer - �You Make Me Feel Like Dancing� � for which he never received credit, a hard lesson in business that drove him to contemplate suicide. All of which was just a prelude to Parker�s own Grammy winning triumph with �Ghostbusters� � and the controversy which followed, in which he stood accused of plagiarizing someone else�s hit. A double-dose of baby mama drama, family loss, and an ill-advised decision to leave his safe haven at Arista Records accelerated his descent from the top of the charts. But Ray Parker proved unsinkable, and along with testimony from his extended musical family � including Cheryl Lynn, Chaka Khan and Clive Davis - he tells �Unsung� the tale of his still-unfolding journey.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Freddie Jackson�s soulful ballads are the stuff of velvet sheets, intimate encounters and rose petaled Jacuzzis. With nine number one hits, including �Rock Me Tonight (For Old Times Sake)� and �You Are My Lady�, Freddie gave voice to sentiments men often struggled to communicate, and women longed to hear. But super-stardom wasn�t all strawberries and whipped cream. Struggling with his weight since childhood, Freddie found his persona at odds with his ballooning figure, while whispers questioning his sexuality swirled amongst fans. Through the 1980s, Freddie helped catapult the Hush productions sound to the R&B forefront - but when the hits ran out, he found himself facing financial ruin. In this revealing episode of �Unsung�, Freddie and his closest collaborators, including Melba Moore and M�lissa Morgan, chart his popular success and his personal struggles.

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: Straight outta Kentucky, Midnight Star rocked the house and dance floor in the early eighties with irresistible jams like �Freak-a-Zoid�, �Operator� , �Slow Jam� and of course, �No Parking on the Dance Floor.� Co-founders Reggie Calloway and Bill Simmons had put the band together while in college, and for the next several years the entire nine piece ensemble literally lived together in a house owned by the Calloway�s mom, who doubled as their manager

programme.title: Unsung
programme.desc: With a gorgeous voice and five octave range, exotic beauty and an intoxicating stage presence, Angela Bofill took the music world by storm. A native New Yorker who grew up in Harlem and the Bronx, she was a trained musician and sophisticated singer who invested ballads like �This Time I�ll be Sweeter,� and her ode to heartbreak, �I Try� with palpable emotion. She could belt out hot dance numbers like �Too Tough�, and gospel-inflected inspirational hymns like �I�m on your Side� with equal aplomb. But after a run of hits in the 1980s, she faded rapidly from view, as record labels trained their sights on a younger generation of video vixens. Bofill soldiered on for two decades, only to be literally silenced by two devastating strokes. Yet she refused to give up her dream, and is gradually returning to the stage, while sharing her inspirational life�s story with hard earned wit and wisdom, on this episode of �Unsung.�
